XOnlineMessageSetProperty

Associates a property with a message.

HRESULT XOnlineMessageSetProperty(
    XONLINE_MSG_HANDLE hMsg,
    WORD wPropTag,
    DWORD dwPropValueSize,
    const VOID *pPropValue,
    DWORD dwAttachmentFlags
);

Parameters

hMsg
[in] Message handle obtained from the XOnlineMessageCreate function.
wPropTag

[in] Property's unique identifier. Can be one of the following values.
Identifier Description
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_VOICE_DATA The message has a voice attachment.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_VOICE_DATA_CODEC Specifies the codec used to play the voice attachment.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_VOICE_DATA_DURATION Specifies the length of the voice attachment.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TEXT The message has a text attachment.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TEXT_LANGUAGE Specifies the language of the text attachment.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_SESSION_ID The property is a session ID.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_SYSTEM_TEXT The message is from the Xbox Live system and has a text attachment
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TEAM_NAME The message property specifies a team name. Used for team recruit messages.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_COMP_DATASET The property is a competition dataset. Used for competition messages.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_COMP_NAME The property contains a competition name. Used for competition messages.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_COMP_START The property contains a competition's start time and date. Used for competition messages.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_COMP_ROUND The property contains a competition round number. Used for competition messages.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_COMP_OPPONENT The property specifies a player's opponent. Used for competition messages.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_COMP_ADMIN The property contains competition administration information. Used for competition messages.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_COMP_REG_CLOSE The property contains the time and date that registration closes. Used for competition messages.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_COMP_PRIVATE_SLOTS The property contains the number of private slots in a competition. Used for competition messages.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_COMP_PUBLIC_SLOTS The property contains the number of public slots in a competition. Used for competition messages.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_COMP_UNITS The property specifies the units of the time interval between rounds. Used for competition messages.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_COMP_INTERVAL The property specifies the time interval between rounds. Used for competition messages.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_COMP_DAYMASK The property specifies day mask for the time interval between rounds. Used for competition messages. For more information, see Scheduling Rounds.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_COMP_DESCRIPTION The property contains a description of the competition. Used for competition messages.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_COMP_URL The property contains the URL of the home page of the competition. Used for competition messages.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_COMP_EVENT_ID The property contains the ID of an event. Used for competition messages. For more information, see Competition Tournament Structure.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_COMP_EVENT_START The property contains the start time and date of an event.

In addition, you can create your own property types with the XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TAG macro. To do so, pass XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TAG as the value for this parameter. Set the first parameter of XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TAG to one of the following values. Set the second parameter of XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TAG to your own custom value.

Identifier Description
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TYPE_NULL The property contains no data.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TYPE_I1 The property value points to 8 bits of data.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TYPE_I2 The property value points to 16 bits of data.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TYPE_I4 The property value points to 32 bits of data.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TYPE_I8 The property value points to 64 bits of data.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TYPE_STRING The property value points to a NULL-terminated wide character string.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TYPE_FILETIME The property value points to a time value.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TYPE_BINARY The property value points to a binary blob that fits in the message details.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TYPE_ATTACHMENT The property value points to a binary blob (or directory path, depending on attachment flags) that will be uploaded to storage during XOnlineMessageSend.
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TYPE_BOOL The property value points to a Boolean value (0=FALSE, 1=TRUE).
XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TYPE_STRING_ID The property value contains a string ID whose text can be requested from the string service.

dwPropValueSize
[in] Size, in bytes, of the property value.
pPropValue
[in] Pointer to the property's actual value.
dwAttachmentFlags

[in] Flags indicating the attachment type. For information on valid flags, see the Xbox Live Messaging section.

Note    These flags are ignored unless the property type is XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TYPE_ATTACHMENT.

Return Values

Returns S_OK if successful, an error code otherwise.

Remarks

A property cannot be added to a message more than once.

Use the XOnlineMessageGetPropType macro to retrieve the property type from the property tag. If the property type is XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TYPE_ATTACHMENT and your title does not pass the XONLINE_MSG_ATTACHMENT_FLAG_DIRECTORY flag through the dwAttachmentFlags parameter, the property value (in the pPropValue parameter) must point to a memory buffer that remains valid until the send operation completes. If the property type is XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TYPE_ATTACHMENT and your title sets the XONLINE_MSG_ATTACHMENT_FLAG_DIRECTORY flag, the property value must point to an ANSI path string to a local directory whose contents must not be changed until the send operation completes.

Adding directory attachments to a message requires additional memory to be allocated from the private heap (allocated when the XOnlineStartup function is called). This allocation can cause the XOnlineMessageSend function operation to fail with the value E_OUTOFMEMORY. To avoid this, make the XOnlineMessageSetProperty call to add the directory attachment the last call after adding all other properties. If you are adding more than one directory attachment, increase the default private heap size by about 45 KB; that is, pass to XOnlineStartup a value of 177152 for the dwMaxPrivatePool member of the XONLINE_STARTUP_PARAMS structure.

When adding an XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TYPE_ATTACHMENT property to a message, the attachment memory buffer or directory must remain valid and unmodified until the XOnlineMessageSend operation for the message completes. Up to 10 unique XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TYPE_ATTACHMENT properties can be added.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xonline.h.

Import Library: Use Xonline.lib.

See Also

XOnlineMessageCreate
